items.headline: Futures Show Positive Momentum Ahead of Market Open on Geopolitical Easing and Earnings Watch
items.summary: U.S. equity futures are indicating a positive open for markets this morning, extending gains from the previous week. Sentiment remains largely optimistic, driven by the news of the Strait of Hormuz reopening and ongoing positive reactions to corporate earnings. Crude oil prices have seen a significant plunge, contributing to the upbeat mood. Investors are also closely monitoring upcoming economic data and major company earnings reports scheduled for the week ahead. Not financial advice.

items.summary: Energy Focus, Inc. saw a significant surge in pre-market trading, leading the gainers list.

items.headline: Strait of Hormuz Reopens, Easing Geopolitical Tensions and Boosting Market Sentiment
items.summary: Reports indicate that Iran has declared the Strait of Hormuz completely open for commercial traffic following a ceasefire, leading to a significant drop in crude oil prices. This development has largely contributed to the positive market sentiment seen in pre-market futures. President Trump has also indicated a peace deal with Iran is 'very close'.

items.headline: Technology and AI Sectors Continue to Show Strength
items.summary: The technology sector, particularly AI-driven companies, is demonstrating continued strength. Nvidia's new AI model has driven gains in quantum computing stocks, while Microsoft advanced following price increases for its Surface line. This highlights ongoing investor interest in innovation and growth areas.

items.headline: Key Earnings Reports on the Horizon
items.summary: Investors are looking ahead to a busy earnings week. Tesla ($TSLA) is scheduled to report on April 22, while tech giants Microsoft ($MSFT), Alphabet ($GOOG), Amazon, and Meta are all set to release their Q1 2026 results on April 29. Daiwa Securities Group ($DSEEY) is also expected to release earnings on April 26.

items.headline: Recent Economic Data Provides Mixed Signals
items.summary: Recent economic data includes a 0.5% fall in U.S. Industrial Production for March, while the Philadelphia Fed Index unexpectedly jumped in April. U.S. Jobless Claims also pulled back more than anticipated. Singapore's Non-Oil Domestic Export Data is due to be released today. The 10-year Treasury yield remains above 4.3%.
